category_title: Young adults with intellectual disability who exhibit passive agreement and off-task behaviors during transitions
scenario_title: Passive agreement during transition goal setting
minimal_context: Michael is participating in an IEP meeting with his teacher and support team to develop his transition goals.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Michael, we would like to develop some goals with you. Your job coach tells me you can do your job well, but that you are not as fast as you could be. Your job coach says you have a tendency to pay more attention to what others are doing and can lose focus on what you need to be doing. Do you think that working on finishing tasks without stopping so much would be a good goal to work on?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: So that is something that you could work on at both school and home. Do you think it is a problem, Michael?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to decide on a goal for your transition plan today. Is it okay if we just go with this one?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Michael, we would like to develop some goals with you. Your job coach tells me you can do your job well, but that you are not as fast as you could be. Your job coach says you have a tendency to pay more attention to what others are doing and can lose focus on what you need to be doing. Do you think that working on finishing tasks without stopping so much would be a good goal to work on?

STUDENT: Sure.

TUTOR: So that is something that you could work on at both school and home. Do you think it is a problem, Michael?

STUDENT: I don’t know. Maybe.

TUTOR: We need to decide on a goal for your transition plan today. Is it okay if we just go with this one?

STUDENT: Sure.

task.content: develop some goals with you. Your job coach tells me you can do your job well, but that you are not as fast as you could be. Your job coach says you have a tendency to pay more attention to what others are doing and can lose focus on what you need to be doing. Do you think that working on finishing tasks without stopping so much would be a good goal to work on?
task.learning_goal: helping young adults with intellectual disability (ID) make authentic decisions about their own goals and behaviors

context.materials: table indicating daily events with columns for notes, interview forms
context.social_setting: IEP meeting with his teacher, job coach, mother, and program supervisor

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When teacher directly suggests a specific goal and asks for agreement
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Agrees automatically with little reflection, provides passive agreement ('Sure')
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: responds automatically in order to please the teacher or parents, reduce the pressure to respond, or avoid cognitive complexity

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to self-evaluate a problem behavior
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: provides non-committal answers, does not see his behavior as concerning
behavior_patterns.internal_state: has a misconception regarding the significance of a behavior
